data.narrative:
Cailabs is a Series C space and aerospace company that has raised €57M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five private peers at identical stage and sector: Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B). Public comps in Space & Aerospace (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trade at a median P/S of 53.2x, but are not deployable here owing to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The five same-stage, same-sector private comparables yield a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.5B. This method reflects current market pricing for pre-IPO space technology and services firms at the Series C stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on $1.5B. Key uncertainties include the target's specific revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to peers—data not available in this assessment—as well as near-term capital efficiency and path to profitability within the sector. The single-method foundation also limits conviction; public comparables remain inaccessible pending revenue disclosure.
